behavior_summary: This script implements a minimal 'U8P_BIGUGLY::look' (inspect) handler for an NPC/object. When the player performs the U8P_BIGUGLY::look/inspect action, the entity emits a single short bark reading "tree ".
After emitting the bark the U8P_BIGUGLY::look process suspends/ends immediately. There is no branching, no menu, no state changes, and no external calls.
Overall the routine appears purely descriptive/emote output intended to provide a short inspect text rather than participate in conversation or game state changes.
notable_control_flow: U8P_BIGUGLY::look: linear sequence — emit Bark("tree ") then suspend/end the U8P_BIGUGLY::look process.
No conditional branches, loops, menu interactions, or flag reads/writes are present in the canonical flow.
open_questions: Is the trailing space in the bark text "tree " intentional (formatting) or an artifact of extraction?
Does the suspend node function as an immediate process termination in this engine context, or could it represent a short wait before resumption? (Inferred as termination/end with medium confidence.)
Are there any external systems (e.g., U8P_BIGUGLY::look-list UI) that additionally record or display this text beyond the bark output? (No evidence in script.)

main_function.name: U8P_BIGUGLY::look
main_function.purpose: Handle the player's U8P_BIGUGLY::look/inspect action for this NPC/object by presenting a short descriptive/emote bark ('tree ') and then ending the U8P_BIGUGLY::look process.
entry_conditions: Invoked by the engine when the player uses the 'U8P_BIGUGLY::look' (inspect) action on the NPC/object (processType 0x0208).

function_map.name: U8P_BIGUGLY::look
function_map.role: inspect/U8P_BIGUGLY::look handler that emits a single bark and suspends the process
